name: Corn Pone
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine the corn meal and salt in a mixing bowl.
2. Gradually add boiling water to the mixture while stirring continuously until a thick batter forms.
3. Melt the lard or fat in the prepared baking pan.
4. Pour the melted fat into the batter, then mix everything together well.
5. Transfer the mixture to the greased baking pan, ensuring it is spread no more than one inch thick.
6. Bake at 350°F for about 50-60 minutes until golden brown and crunchy on the crust.
The pone will rise only slightly; to achieve a looser texture similar to cornbread, add 2 teaspoons of baking powder. Traditionally, corn bread and corn pone were staples in the mountain diet, often enjoyed with greens (known as "salit greens"), meat, and cold milk from the springhouse, making for hearty meals where friends are always welcome.
